title: linum tauricum subsp. serbicum
intro: Linum tauricum subsp. serbicum, also known as flax-leaved linum, is a plant species belonging to the Linaceae family, which is native to the Balkan Peninsula. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to its linolenic acid and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Linum tauricum subsp. serbicum include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as supporting the cardiovascular system and immune system. The plant is often prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and its seeds and leaves can be used in various herbal preparations, such as salves and creams, to harness its medicinal properties.
